questions.question: Who is the author of the first part, 'His First Flight'?
questions.answer: The first part was written by Liam O’Flaherty.

questions.question: Why was the young seagull afraid to fly?
questions.answer: He was afraid because he felt certain that his wings would never support him and he would fall into the sea.

questions.question: How long had the young seagull been alone on his ledge?
questions.answer: He had been alone for twenty-four hours.

questions.question: What did the seagull’s mother do to force him to fly?
questions.answer: She flew near him with a piece of fish in her beak but stopped just out of his reach to tempt him into diving.

questions.question: What was the seagull's first flight experience like once he dove?
questions.answer: Initially, he felt a monstrous terror, but soon his wings spread outwards, and he felt the wind against his feathers.

questions.question: Who is the author of 'The Black Aeroplane'?
questions.answer: The second part was written by Frederick Forsyth.

questions.question: Which plane was the narrator flying in 'The Black Aeroplane'?
questions.answer: He was flying his old Dakota DS 088.

questions.question: From where to where was the narrator flying?
questions.answer: He was flying from France back to England.

questions.question: What did the narrator see in front of him after he was 150km from Paris?
questions.answer: He saw huge, black storm clouds that looked like black mountains standing in front of him.

questions.question: Why didn't the narrator fly around the storm clouds?
questions.answer: He didn't have enough fuel to fly around them to the north or south, and he was eager to get home for breakfast.

questions.question: What happened to the instruments in the Dakota when it entered the storm?
questions.answer: The compass stopped working and was turning round and round; the radio was also dead.

questions.question: What was unusual about the black aeroplane that appeared in the storm?
questions.answer: It had no lights on its wings and appeared suddenly next to the narrator in the dark clouds.

questions.question: How did the pilot of the black aeroplane help the narrator?
questions.answer: The pilot waved his hand, signaling the narrator to follow him through the storm.

questions.question: Where did the narrator land safely?
questions.answer: He landed safely on a runway at an airport.

questions.question: What did the woman in the control center tell the narrator about the other plane?
questions.answer: She said there was no other plane on the radar except his Dakota.

questions.question: What did the young seagull's siblings do that he couldn't?
questions.answer: They had already flown away the day before, successfully mastering the art of flight.

questions.question: What was the 'first catch' of the seagull’s older brother?
questions.answer: His older brother caught his first herring and devoured it.

questions.question: What did the seagull's father do while the seagull stayed on the ledge?
questions.answer: His father was preening the feathers on his white back.

questions.question: What did the seagull do when he landed on the green sea?
questions.answer: He dropped his legs to stand on it, but they sank; then his belly touched it and he floated.

questions.question: What was the pilot’s mood at the start of 'The Black Aeroplane'?
questions.answer: He was happy to be alone high up above the sleeping countryside, dreaming of his holiday.

questions.question: Which control station did the pilot contact first?
questions.answer: He contacted Paris Control.

questions.question: Why was the narrator frightened again after following the black plane for some time?
questions.answer: Because he realized he only had enough fuel in his last tank to fly for five or ten minutes more.

questions.question: What is the theme of 'His First Flight'?
questions.answer: The theme is overcoming fear through necessity and the importance of self-confidence.

questions.question: What does the 'Black Aeroplane' symbolize?
questions.answer: It symbolizes a mystery or a 'miraculous' help that comes from within one's own courage or subconscious during a crisis.

questions.question: How did the seagull’s family celebrate his first flight?
questions.answer: They were screaming, praising him, and offering him scraps of dog-fish.
